dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A frightful night... cold, and dark, and everything. You've spoken certain vows, and as the Moon is our witness, I too have spoken mine. Now we may clash in earnest finally.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: In earnest... so you have been holding back against me. You said you'd push me to my limits and beyond.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our limits grow along with us. I know mine well enough by now; do you know yours? I am eager to find out for myself!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You look upon the Oath of the Unseen night after night, but: Do you understand as yet the lesson it imparts?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Fear manifests at night. Either we give in to that fear, or we become its cause.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Either give in to fear or become its cause. Your words, not mine, though I concur with them. Let us become that fear for one another once again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Whether 'tis wise to speak the Vow of Rivals is for you to decide, but... I can see the wisdom in rescinding that one. I'd not wish to face me at my best, either.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I may not be finished with that Vow just yet, Headmistress. I wonder what else you're holding back, if you possess such power you suppress by will of Night alone.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Do wonder, by all means. And should you require more of an advanced test of your aptitude, I shall be waiting.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Ah, you spoke the Vow of Rivals once more! I didn't even have to ask. Was it the added fierceness of this test that compelled you or the change of locale?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps both, Headmistress. I want to keep things lively and to keep making the most of these tests of yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Keeping things lively may be difficult in this realm but I take your meaning. I have asked you to hold nothing back against me... and this way, I can best heed my own advice.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You're angry; let it out. Anger's not to be suppressed, nor misdirected. To be angry... is to be alive.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Ankh Scepter of Anubis... I've seen it in my travels, but to see it so close to where we reside is unusual indeed. Perhaps it can sense all the Shades of the dead nearby.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The one who wielded this scepter also presides over the dead, if I understand correctly. How many gods of the dead are there?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Who can say? There are a lot of dead, and more accumulating all the time. Some shall be looking to that Scepter of yours for guidance. Show them the way.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You are preoccupied with that Arachne's curse! I know about it, yes. The remedy is no straightforward feat.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nor is my task, yet here we are, night after night. If there's a way to aid my friend, I wish to know of it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Athena is the one to persuade; so long as she wishes for the curse to remain, it shall. That, and you must discover who created it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I appreciate your candor with Arachne, Headmistress. You must have known full well that she would not forgive you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Far easier to beg forgiveness than to dole it out. As you grow longer in years, you, too, shall have relationships that cannot be repaired.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You wear one of those silken dresses, always with a vivid hue. I know whose handiwork they are. This one quite suits you, though it is unlikely to withstand the heat...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your aura is different. Stronger. You have been studying the Arcana. Meditating at your Altar to prepare for your attempts.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Each Card reveals to me a different strength. If I steady myself and concentrate, then it's as though I become one with whomever they depict. If only I could focus on them all.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: To focus, by its nature, is to choose. Come, demonstrate to me this newfound understanding of yours...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Altar in our training grounds is for your benefit, yet you would face me whilst ignoring the Arcana! An oversight, or bit of cheekiness?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Merely a bit of deception with which to take you off your guard, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Hah! I think not. I'm fully on my guard, and suggest you be on yours.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: How fares the goddess of the hunt? By now these woods must feel a second home to her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sister Artemis appears quite well, so much as possible of late. If anything, she seems in better spirits than before.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Good. She, Selene, you, and I have all prepared for this contingency, and now 'tis time we shine... particularly you and I, right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Athena deigned to grace these woods of ours, has she? Perhaps a mere projection of her power now that you and she have built up more of a working relationship.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Her Gorgon Amulet lets her locate me almost anywhere, though she can never stay for very long. I hate to inconvenience her.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: She would not bother if she deemed you unworthy. Whilst her power may be limited beyond Olympus, I shall not underestimate it here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Now that's a sight, you bearing down on me with that gigantic Moonstone Axe! I wasn't certain you'd prefer that one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zorephet is weightier than the other Arms. But having an axe such as this in woods such as these has been convenient, overall.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well, much as I hate to inconvenience you, I must. Although, that monstrous thing suggests this oughtn't take too long...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That little cat there by your side, I noticed her within our Crossroads recently. Your power must be great indeed, to have persuaded her to follow you...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Toula did take some convincing, Headmistress. But she seems to appreciate my company! At least, most of the time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Moody creatures, cats; born huntresses but uncontrollable. I almost fear to see just what this little one can do! With hounds, at least I know exactly where I stand.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis not the typical slim figure of the Moonstone Axe. Ah! The Aspect of Charon! He has since taken up the simple oar.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: So this is why Lord Charon has such definition in his arms. Do you suppose he used to row his boat with Zorephet?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps he did, or hewed off a few heads. If Nyx and the Three Fates entrusted the protection of the Underworld to a certain few, they could have chosen worse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, I'm concerned Chronos may find us. I first encountered him not far beyond these woods...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: There are so many passageways to Erebus, 'tis exceedingly unlikely he shall find the way. You face a greater risk than that right now...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our enemy's strength even exceeds my own. We're both Titans, yes, Chronos and I, but he is my elder... as I am yours. What say you?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: His age doesn't mean anything to me. The old are seldom wise, and often weak. And, he did not study under you, Headmistress. Only I have.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Hah! Just so. He is an arrogant old fool. The impression of wisdom comes with age more than the real thing. Remember that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll get him, Headmistress. I swear it. All that you've taught, I'll bring to bear. And no matter the obstacles that stand in the way, Chronos shall fall by my hand.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I require no convincing, Melinoë, although perhaps you do. However much I've taught, what's important is you're a capable student.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: I have to be more than that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've vanquished Chronos... everything you've trained me to do. Yet still you stand in my way. Still more to learn even now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Now and always. That tinge of frustration you expressed? I would see you rid yourself of it, in your attempts to rid yourself of me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our enemy surely is beginning to wonder how you are able to thwart him. Do leave him in the dark. The less he knows of us, the better.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos seems to think my family bond with him is why I'm able to resist. He hasn't made the connection that you're the one who's prepared me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have prepared yourself. 'Tis good that you are drawing his attention and his wrath, whilst I remain unseen. We slowly undermine his strength... and through our efforts, buy your mountain relatives more time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your staff takes on an unexpected form. Last I saw it thus was on a distant isle in the seas above. Where the enchantress Circe plied her trade.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is the form Descura took with her. I feel a greater sense of calm and focus now, as though her own experience fused with mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Circe knew Descura's next bearer would need it for a task of some import. Thus she lent some of her strength to complement your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: If you lack confidence, then how are you to manifest your will? Be certain of your heart's desire.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A flash of silver... Lim and Oros. So, the Sister Blades availed themselves to you. The other Nocturnal Arms shall follow their lead, I expect.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Nyx entrusted them to you to safeguard the realm as a last resort. But, this cannot possibly be how they were meant to be used...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: And whyever not? To safeguard the realm, you shall have to clear a wide variety of obstacles standing in your way. I am but one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A chill swept through the air as you approached. Unseasonably cold; though perhaps it is as Demeter desires. She aids you, does she not?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She does. She knew me for her granddaughter at once. Have you had opportunity to speak with her, about me and all that's transpired?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: No; I've been a bit preoccupied. At least here, Demeter shall be able to cool her wrath against me for keeping you a secret for so long...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I sense that Demeter is with you once again. I suspected she would either be among the first to lend support, or to refuse.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Could you not have told her about me, Headmistress? To have lost her daughter and grandchildren... I cannot imagine her sorrow.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Twas a painful choice to leave her in the dark. She would have gone to war immediately; but our task requires patience, as you know.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Not seen you here of late, Melinoë. And you've not ventured to the surface either recently. Your dreams have called to you instead, is that not so?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I heard from Lord Hypnos again after his rather sudden departure. He's helping me explore my dreams, yes... confront my lingering fears from doing this night after night.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Dreams rooted in reality are the most troubling sort. Well, as long as you do not remain forever trapped in flights of fancy! I have come to enjoy these occasional clashes.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Trust in your training, ere you reach your destination at the House. That and your connection to Chronos affords you some degree of control over his abilities... an unwitting inheritance he gave to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My knowledge of dream, his command over Time... a potent combination, but one I know I must approach with a great deal of caution and respect.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Approaching matters with caution and respect shall rarely lead you astray. I shall await word of whatever it is you discover... and whether our plans may come to pass.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: To the abyss of the subconscious I descend... remember well those words and those that follow, lest the thrill of battle cloud your memory.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To the abyss of the subconscious I descend. Night and Darkness, guide me to my blood beyond the grasp of Time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A potent prayer, although here it shall avail you not. Perhaps you soon shall have the opportunity to make the invocations in the proper place and time.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've told my brother of what Chronos has done... but he expressed reluctance about having to destroy a member of our family. He even... suggested Father may be partly to blame.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A complication. Your brother never was keen on taking direction, especially from your father. And now he's filling your own head with doubt. We have no recourse here, Melinoë. Persuade him, or all shall be for naught.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He also asked me what exactly happened between Chronos and my father, and... I realized I didn't know. Does anyone? This war he's waged... it isn't merely over who should rule.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Their precise history is of no bearing to our present state. In war, one would be wise to not give undue credit to one's enemies. If you spend all your time so deep in thought, then you shall hesitate to act... and your moment shall pass!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Tell me, ere we clash again: Has your brother come to understand yet the necessity of his participation in your task?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's been a bit of a struggle. He's found a suitable weapon in our father's spear... but he hasn't granted me its essence yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Then tell him what he needs to hear, if that is what it takes to spur him on. I do not like having to count on him any more than you, but... his cooperation is an absolute necessity.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I need more than his cooperation... I need his trust. I'm not going to deceive him, if that's what you suggest.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I suggest nothing. The task is yours, as are the methods you employ to achieve it. But, achieve it!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Zagreus and I finally reached an understanding. He knows what needs to be done... but, I wanted to ask you something, Headmistress. With Chronos, in the past... there's truly no other course but to destroy him?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Oh, blood and darkness, he's got to you, hasn't he? Your brother at least, if not Chronos himself. Why are you asking me such things when you have come so close to the completion of your task, Melinoë?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Well, for one... we're on the verge of destroying Time itself. For all you've taught me, I've never fully understood the repercussions this would have.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We cannot know such repercussions ere they come to pass. Our charge, your charge, is to stop Chronos. Whatever it takes. I trust you shall require no additional reminders?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That form of Ygnium is rather fetching! You could always decorate the Crossroads with those lights should they not prove sufficiently dependable for your task.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The Aspect of Eos is not as delicate as it appears. Though I'll admit I half-expected that this Aspect of the Flames would be your own.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Nonsense. If I myself wielded those very torches, you would not be bearing them. Now come remind me what the rosy flickering of Dawn can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What can we do for the wretched souls wandering lost in Erebus, Headmistress? An afterlife of lashing out in impotent rage...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We can achieve our task. The souls of Erebus ought have been placed somewhere within your father's realm, but all that ceased once Chronos took the throne. Go, wrest it back from him.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This grove is already regrown since last we clashed. For all the death in Erebus, life flourishes as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Death and life; the two are intricately intertwined. These woods shall easily survive our clashes here. What about you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Eugh...! Whatever foul Keepsake you decided to bring along for good fortune this eve, 'tis a horrid thing. Which sort of comrade would reward you thus?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It... was a gift from Eris, Headmistress. I know the effect isn't strictly to my benefit, or anyone's... but I just thought...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: ...That Eris was doing you a favor? Or has any sense of purpose other than to sow disorder where she can? You two make a strange match, Melinoë. So shall this be.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Much is expected of you. So you must learn to bear that burden with grace.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You must wear that veil so as not to betray your disappointment, Headmistress. All these failures yet you still do not admonish me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Oh silence, Melinoë. For your setbacks I've boundless patience, but for your petulance, I've none. You ought find a better catalyst for such inner turmoils, as I lack sympathy for them!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: There's not much glory in confronting adversaries one to one. I myself invoke Shades of the dead to alter the odds. Some may well favor you, instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps if I brought with me a legion of Animal Familiars next time, Headmistress? Not that any beast would bare its fangs at you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You jest, yet a Familiar may be of considerable benefit given all that you have to do. A possibility to think upon, once you are rid of me...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lately I dreamt of Father once again, when Chronos came for him, and you took me to safety. Except this time, I saw more than before...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Twas no mere dream, of course. What more did you learn whilst you lingered thus?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Chronos demanded Father tell him how to locate the Three Fates. Chronos hates them, or feels threatened by them. He was searching for them all this time... and must have found them finally.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Blast... Hermes suspected that this was the case, but has been unable to prove it beyond doubt. For now, let us proceed as though the Fates themselves oppose us; as though we've nobody to count on save ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I think I know where they are now, Headmistress. The Three Fates, I mean. I may be able to find them at long last.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well, that is quite the development! Of all your grandfather's wrongdoings, making off with the Fates is one of the most brazen. Where is it that you think they are?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Not far below, or at least, that's where I think I can reach them. I wonder what they're like... the children of Nyx all seem quite different from one to the next.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: If you are close to locating them, then you needn't wonder for much longer. What I have come to know about them is, there is no telling what they have in store for any of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Decided to take a break from all your recent outings to the surface, have you now?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's much to be done, both above and below. You always say the greatest teacher is out there. But I always seem to find her here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I taught you nothing of such flattery. One of Odysseus' tricks, perhaps...! Let's see what others you have learned.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You brought some company along! An unassuming choice. Your amphibious friend does not appear to have capacity to sway the outcome here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I never trust appearances entirely, Headmistress, and know neither do you. This must be an exciting night for Frinos.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We are not here to entertain your woodland friends, Melinoë. Pray they don't get underfoot.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Wheel trinket I bestowed. I once used it to hone my craft. May it now help you do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This wheel, a labyrinth with no beginning and no end... pathways that circle back upon themselves. Like Erebus...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Like life itself! Three stages, intertwined. Let us go through the stages now.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Again you bear the Wheel I gave to you; rather, that I held for you ere you were ready, for it was always yours. A means of focusing your magickal acuity, if nothing else.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's much more. A never-ending labyrinth with three distinct sides... a reminder of our home, and the limits of our power.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Crossroads may serve as something of a home to you, but most certainly it is not your home. It is that labyrinth in which you are trapped. Break free!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Ah, the Frost Mane... I felt a chill in the air moments prior, which must have come from that Aspect. The land in which I first encountered it was the coldest I have thus experienced.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've been to this other realm of the dead where the goddess Hel resides? I can't imagine any place beneath the Earth being colder than the heights of Olympus.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The world is vast. Your grandmother may govern the turning of the seasons, but there are other goddesses out there who have a very icy touch. You are one of them right now.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I heard from Hermes, Headmistress. He gave me the impression that Olympus needs my aid. What of my task?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your task remains, as does the adversary whom you face. We shall discuss the matter with Hermes elsewhere. We have other matters first.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Hermes urged me to address a certain matter with you here. However, my view is you are unprepared for the surface, as of yet.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Meaning you won't lift the wards barring the path to the surface. What if I remove them myself? I've seen how you do it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Then you'll be more prepared than you are now. Till then, remember your objective lies below. Past me.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps some evening I shall reach this point so quickly that I'll slip by before you arrive, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I've no intent to permit any such possibility, not even if you've the swiftness of Hermes speeding you on. Though, why don't we get to it, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Each hidden Aspect of the Arms of Night is in your grasp, at least the ones that had been set aside for you. What have you surmised about their bearers?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: That they're all gods or goddesses of death or destruction. Some past, some future, others perhaps contemporaries but of realms other than our own. But, where...?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our realm is hidden; theirs may be as well. Besides, for as much of the world as you've seen, 'tis but a narrow swath. You have eternity to meet these other gods, or not.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Look at that prim posture! We're in the presence of majestic Hecuba herself...! How fares my favored hound?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: She's very well, and a most-capable companion to have at my side. Though I hate to have to strain her loyalties like this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Come, you want to see what she shall do! Her loyalty is absolute. I permitted her to serve you as she serves me! Let's all now bare our fangs.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You cannot beat me with anxiety, you know. Still your senses, and prepare yourself. Know you the purpose of these bouts of ours?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: To complete my training. Each clash between us is an opportunity to learn and grow strong.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You're partially correct. For our training shall never be complete. Yet, onward we must strive.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Using the Argent Skull of Revaal, now, are we? You're becoming a most-capable necromancer, to have earned its trust.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Was this once a living creature's skull, Headmistress? Only to be infused with silver and enchanted as an instrument of Night...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That is rather a personal question, considering all evidence at hand! Perhaps Revaal herself shall reveal to you the answer in due course.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Argent Skull must have a sense of humor that it took on such a form for Medea. She professes a great love for skulls in general, yet her own Aspect scarce resembles one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Lady Medea still must have been pleased to use Revaal. Though, I've wondered why the Nocturnal Arms take on such different-looking forms for different bearers.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Must there always be a reason? We all have different aspects of ourselves, which we may reveal or not to those we meet. Go on, now: Summon up some of Medea's wrath.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The air hangs heavily about you here tonight. Ah! 'Tis from the scrap of something that Medea gave to you. A gift more than a curse, I trust?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Her Keepsake, yes. A bit of fleece... sheep roam about Ephyra, but none with coats as black and brilliant as this. It's something special, I can tell.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Indeed it is, as fleeces go, at least. Medea is not quick to accept others into her circle; bit of a black sheep herself. But, aren't we all?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Now in whose guise is Ygnium this time? If you are comfortable answering. 'Tis not your Aspect of the Umbral Flames, that much is clear.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This Aspect is of Moros, Headmistress. But he has not yet used the Flames. Perhaps in knowing first... we become instrumental in bringing him into our fold.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis how the Fates do like to operate. As soon as we know what the future holds, we may try to resist... only to stumble into bringing it about.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Do I detect an artifact from Moros? Something to bring Doom swiftly to your foes by forestalling your own. Seems rather handy, that.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm certain Lord Moros means you no offense. But his Keepsake is of benefit to me, and I'm to take every advantage that I can.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Correct. 'Tis no wonder Doom has come to stay with us for now. His presence lingers over every action that we take...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Crow Cutters of the Morrigan... now there's a dangerous combination. Much like the phantom goddesses who wielded them.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Sister Artemis described the Morrigan as another threefold goddess of shadow, but of a different age and place... I always thought you were one-of-a-kind, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Or perhaps the Morrigan were so enamored of my peerless technique that I became a source of influence! What other explanation could there be?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Does anyone besides me ever happen on you in this glade? Perhaps some evening Artemis or someone may appear instead.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: This clearing, rest assured, is ours alone; these woods are vast enough. Yet I see everyone who passes through. No matter how clandestine they may think they are...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: What is that foul artifact you bear? Ah! An Eye of Nemesis. Warding against those harboring ill will. The sheer irony of it, coming from her.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I mean nothing untoward, Headmistress. The Eye is indiscriminate in its effect, and makes me match more strongly with my strongest foes.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a wonder this Eye does not cause the two of us to combust on the spot! Then take your vengeance, if you can!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Now that is a substantial armament you have... the Rock Lion Mace, is it? Slow and unwieldy... certainly not my style.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It works for me. And not only is it terribly destructive, I find it quite effective for relieving stress! Makes a very satisfying clang. You'll see in a moment... or hear, I suppose.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Will I, now? It seems the Aspect of Nergal bestows in its bearer a prodigious sense of humor to accompany the oversized mallet! Come then and clang away.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: There's a particularly special Aspect that you have... the Nocturnal Arms had to meet a certain standard, and who better to approve that standard than Night herself?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: From everything I know of Nyx, she doesn't seem the sort to physically come to blows. She needed such a weapon to bring the Underworld to order?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: She needed such a weapon to be feared! But, that was long ago... Night was not as widely known as she is now. Yet the Black Coat remains in perfect working order...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Blood and darkness, I can sense you've those accursed Knuckle Bones on you. I shall have to thank Odysseus for providing such a gift...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My Keepsake? I'm surprised these have any effect on you, Headmistress. I thought for certain you enchanted them yourself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Odysseus has known his share of witches besides us. Well, I'm braced for impact; the Knuckle Bones strike first, and then, we fight.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Olympus has emboldened you, and made you strong. Yet, 'tis a fleeting strength; one false step, and I could wipe it all away.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'll take a fleeting strength over no strength at all. We take every advantage that avails itself.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Just so. Then come and show me how you would make use of such advantages.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You've a particular objective to achieve here, haven't you? Seen what must be done within the facets of the Pitch-Black Stone.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A trial, on top of a trial. This is one of the infinite possibilities that Chaos anticipated. I chose this path, but... it was already there for me to find.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis very much disquieting to know the choices that we make might all be preordained to some extent. Nevertheless, Night's will is to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That Pitch-Black Stone in our training grounds... I've sensed it ever since your incantation. The subtle influence of Chaos on this realm.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Our goals are intertwined. When I gaze upon the Stone, I begin to see different paths and possibilities in every facet. Some leading closer to our victory.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis very much disquieting to know the choices that we make might all be preordained to some extent. Nevertheless, Night's will is to be done.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I sense the changes in the night; in you. Your strength has waned significantly. Chaos wishes to see the outcome of this particular arrangement, then?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Another trial, Headmistress. My magick may be weakened, but you taught me never to depend on it entirely.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: And whether you took such lessons to heart, we shall imminently see. As Selene be our witness above, so shall Chaos below.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You bear the Aspect of the Queen. 'Tis a reminder, perhaps, that not even the Nocturnal Arms are infallible, lest Queen Persephone would never have been taken.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: What was Mother supposed to do... fight off Chronos herself when he ambushed everybody in the House?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Precisely my point. Your mother did not even have Revaal by then. Even the most vigilant of us lets down their guard from time to time... though never in this glade.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Why, if it isn't little Gale scurrying about your feet! Ah, how the tables have turned. Her vengeance finally is nigh!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You jest, Headmistress, but I put poor Gale in a terrible position here, to have to square against you. Can't she refuse to be a part of this?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: She is to do your bidding when required, and besides, I do think she is going to enjoy this. I let you go all-out; same goes for her.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Are you better prepared this time? Look at yourself, you are too calm. Suppressing your anger for the illusion of control.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've taught me patience, Headmistress. But now you ask me to lash out at you in rage. Which shall it be?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Give me both: rage, which you can control. Leave the serenity to our Sister the Moon, and fight me properly!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have a raven friend with you! Birds seldom take to Erebus, if they can even make their way! A spy, I thought at first, but no. Perhaps Selene sent him down to you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This is Raki, whom I found in a secluded glade nearby all on his own. I know better than to trust inherently, though felt a strong connection to this one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I likewise sense no ill intent from him. Our Familiars may take on a variety of forms, no different from our friends or enemies. Let's see how well this one keeps up!

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress, why is it that we have to keep this up? I've passed your test repeatedly; we're each needed elsewhere.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You may be strong but not yet strong enough! And evidently quite impatient, too. Shall you attempt to talk Chronos out of a fight as well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Of course not, but... I could be halfway to the Mourning Fields by now, if not for this.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Alas that things don't always go our way. Go on now, vent the anger that you feel! We each have much of it to spare.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Set your respect for me aside whilst here. You must give no such quarter to our enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: But you are not my enemy, Headmistress. How am I to treat you as one?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: How? Allow me to give you a demonstration, then.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Shall we continue with these clashes for eternity? 'Twould seem there are sufficient possibilities of Chronos to keep you occupied that long.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I'm in no hurry, Hecate. Besides, if I once dreaded having to confront you here, I came to enjoy it at some point.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I would prefer more frequent victories but I came to enjoy it in kind. Let us continue, then... for however long it takes.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Sister Selene aided you in getting here to me. But have you learned to harness her power?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: On that account, I would prefer to demonstrate than speak. With your permission, of course, Headmistress.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Permission granted. Now, show me what the Moon can do once in your grasp.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The might of the Moon. That you have come to bear Selene's favored Aspect of the Black Coat ultimately comes as no surprise, though I am pleased to see it.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Did you ever see Selene wield Xinth herself? She always seems so collected that I have trouble picturing her battling head-on.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well, she was never one for battle-cries and such, but yes, I've witnessed her seldom-seen temper... and 'tis something to behold. And as for yours, here you can let it out.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Ah, I stand in the presence of the Purifying Grace of Shiva the Destroyer... of Shiva's Aspect, anyhow. Has that destructive force thus served you well?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You could say that. Of all the Aspects that I've seen, this one seems capable of inflicting the most catastrophic harm in a single strike. Shiva must be very powerful.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Some say Shiva is both a creator and a destroyer... not unlike your grandmother, perhaps, with her cycle of death and rebirth. Show me what you can do with such power!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You spoke the Oath of the Unseen, and would enact Night's Testament this eve. I sensed the growing Fear. How much of it is yours?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A healthy fear, no more, no less, Headmistress. I'm honored that you granted me the right to take the Oath. Assuming it was your doing, rather than Commander Schelemeus.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Best not to speculate too much. 'Twas your own doing most of all. Then, face me on this terror-stricken eve! You have made certain vows, and shall experience the consequence.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Decided to forgo the Oath for now? A wise move, like as not. Or, evidence of some restraint and circumspection on your part, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My path is treacherous enough as it is; why make such Vows as would reduce my chances of success?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis through adversity that we grow strong! But to your point, you face adversity enough. The Oath is there to guarantee you always shall.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Fear in the air... 'tis almost palpable tonight. You have made many Vows this eve. Feeling so very bold, or merely curious to see what shall transpire?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: A little of each, Headmistress? Perhaps I made a terrible mistake. It's fortunate the Vows contained within the Oath can be undone... unlike most vows.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: All vows can be undone. But yes, the Oath is oddly forgiving in this way; its Vows, never strictly binding. Perhaps so that each evening may be interestingly different from the last.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Testament of Night requires my defeat specifically, is that not so? The Titan Lord can wait; the key to making the Nocturnal Arms more powerful stands right in front of you.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You know much more about the Oath of the Unseen than I, Headmistress. So why don't you tell me what it desires.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Oh, I just did! Perhaps when you come to be my age, you too shall find a new connection to the Oath. For now, come earn the Nightmare that you seek.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The greatest teacher is out there, and evidently taught you quite a bit. You're able to surpass me here consistently. But has this proved to be enough...?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: There's always more to be learned, Headmistress; you taught me that yourself. If you believe that I'm prepared, why hesitate?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Because your path toward mastery is yours alone; when you decide you are prepared for more, remember that the Oath of the Unseen is there for you. As am I.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a particularly quiet night. Observe the silence for a moment, ere we begin our little test of just how capable you have become.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It does seem unusually still, and the Moon wanes. An ill portent, Headmistress? Our Shades should check up on your hounds...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: No need, the hounds fend mostly for themselves. Like as not, they use this time to rest; as you'd be wise to do, now and again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I didn't mean to keep you waiting long, Headmistress. Or do you always arrive at just the right instant?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: A bit of solitude here hardly is a waste of time; I never merely wait. I've more matters to attend to in these woods, besides. But first...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We are alone; nary the baying of a single hound is to be heard. Selene high above shall have to be sufficient for an audience.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Black Coat you now possess... surrounding you as though 'twas forged with you in mind. I feared that it was broken and beyond repair, but... it was merely waiting.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Waiting for me... I've never wielded anything like it. Xinth doesn't exactly listen to my thoughts, but heeds my every instinct.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Not your every instinct; the Arms of Night have a singular purpose, which they go about each in their own peculiar respect. You are to execute that purpose without fail.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Devil Sparks of Supay... flickering flames from the deepest, hottest recesses of the Earth, now in your own possession. They act of their own accord, do they not?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: This Aspect is unusual in that regard. I know the Nocturnal Arms each have a will of their own to an extent, but with the Aspect of Supay, it's unmistakable.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well then, I suppose if you prevail in this clash, the glory ought to go to those Flames rather than you! Come, let's see some sparks fly.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Hmm. I was beginning to think you had grown so fond of the surface that you would not be coming back here anytime soon.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Our task takes me to the very ends of the Earth. I have unfinished business there as well as here. I'm much more comfortable in these environs, though.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Comfortable? You should be fully on your guard, and braced for battle! Let's see how comfortable this is for both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I know your task is difficult, and also know you have it in you to succeed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your task may be an honor, but the wisest whom you know shall not envy you for it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: There you are. I cannot stay for long. Just long enough to put you to the test.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I got here as quickly as I could, Headmistress. And still you had to wait.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: To wait is the practice of patience. Now, on to other practices, I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I trust the dark of Erebus was not too much to handle with the ever-burning Flames of Ygnium to light your path?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: No, Headmistress, it was not. Although, I've much to learn in how to wield these Flames.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Then you have come to the right place. Show me how you make the shadows dance.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Umbral Flames of Ygnium, at your command! You use them in your own way; not merely copy my technique.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You always said once I was ready to brandish the Flames, that I ought wield them with my intuition as my guide.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Just so. I always favored these old Flames of mine, but... as to whether you and yours truly connect, we'll have to see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Has the thin air upon that mountain made you mad, Melinoë? Confronting Typhon of all creatures face to face! Hermes told me everything. Save what was going through your head.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I was not about to turn my back on my family, especially not after having come so far. What should I have done, flee at the first sight of that monster? We flee only as a last resort.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That we do. And you had best keep reminding yourself of it ere you confront the Father of All Monsters again. Do not take unnecessary risks, or lose sight of the task.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Has the thin air upon that mountain made you mad, Melinoë? Confronting Typhon of all creatures face to face! Hermes told me everything. Save what was going through your head.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: He's much, much larger than any other foe I've faced, but that doesn't make him invincible. I suspected he'd have a difficult time contending with a small, quick adversary.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I would commend you for the outcome were I not so utterly aghast. The Father of All Monsters may not be quite so easy to surprise ere you confront him again. Do not take unnecessary risks, or lose sight of the task!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Had your fill of Olympus for a while? Surpassing me here must be far less burdensome than having to gouge all of Typhon's eyes.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: It's positively pleasant by comparison. But I'll get back to dealing with the Father of All Monsters soon enough.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Then gather what strength you can in the meantime. For now, the Underworld awaits; but I yet hold the key.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: What's that you're brandishing? Ahh, the Nocturnal Arms begin to show their myriad colors. The silver you well know is but one.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Each of the Aspects of the Arms of Night represents a bearer from another time. I'm still learning the full extent of what they can do.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: And I suspect you shall continue doing so. If nothing else, this ought to make things further interesting here for the both of us.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Each night, I wonder which of your forms I shall confront. Have you considered why so many different Aspects of the Nocturnal Arms are at your disposal?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: The bearers of the Arms safeguard the Underworld, but... I don't think they all could wield Aspects other than their own. Perhaps something about my task requires it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Or perhaps the Arms have come to have affinity for you; not unlike a close companion who entrusts you with a secret that is never to be shared! Do not betray their confidence.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our craft leaves little room for doubt, yet some always remains; and even a little can be far too much.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You almost had me last we clashed, although a narrow loss is nonetheless a loss. I expect you to follow through to victory this time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Welcome to the edge of Erebus. Well done, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress Hecate. I could not have reached this point without your guidance.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: And, in turn, you shall not pass this point without a demonstration of your capability.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My capability? You'd have us face each other here... even though my task has already begun?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your training shall never be complete. And I would know if you stand any chance at all against our enemy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You reached the edge of Erebus already. You have surpassed my expectations, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress Hecate. For all your guidance, and the compliment.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Twas not a compliment, merely a point of fact. Though I would see firsthand a demonstration of your capability.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My capability? You'd have us face each other here... even though my task has already begun?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your training shall never be complete. And I would know if you stand any chance at all against our enemy.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have grown physically quite strong... but spiritually, more is to be done. The Altar of Ashes; there is an incantation that may make your nightly meditations at it better spent.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have survived against me, and for that, you're owed a gift. May you learn well through it, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Do bring an Animal Familiar along next time, if but to keep things livelier for me! We have the means to keep them safe, even in scuffles such as these.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: Faith of Familiar Spirits... this should work on Frinos, or perhaps...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: One further secret of the Oath of the Unseen I shall reveal now. When you feel suitably prepared, consider this. Then perhaps we shall clash under a new set of circumstances...

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: The secret to the Vow of Rivals...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: To bear each of the Nocturnal Arms is a terrible responsibility. You've only begun to realize their nature; a certain incantation shall deepen your understanding.

endLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
endLines.text: The Aspects of Night and Darkness...

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: It seems the pathways into Erebus are not as well-protected as they used to be. I take it this here is the individual who pitted you against me, is this not?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Headmistress! Headmistress, fight him, fight! Fight back!!

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: This headmistress of yours has considerable knowledge, to have thus concealed you... but she is weary, such that Time caught up with her at last. She hears not a word you say.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unhand her, wretch. Unhand her, or I'll tear you to pieces.

dialogueLines.speaker: NPC_Chronos_01
dialogueLines.text: My own children tore me to pieces once before. Look where that got them! Whoever this is, perhaps she shall be more cooperative than you, although I am not particularly optimistic; nor should you be. Good evening!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Forgive my bluntness but you're in a rather sorry-looking state, Witch. 'Tis unfair for me to fight you whilst I am at my best and you are not.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Life, however, is unfair, is it not, Headmistress? Here lies a lesson to be learned I think.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Just so. Pray you fare as capably in battle as in speech. Now commence!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You've suffered injuries, Melinoë. You know that I can cure them for you, and you know that I shall not. Not here. It brings me no pleasure.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My injuries are teachings, Headmistress. I could have been more careful getting here. Now I face the consequence.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Correct! Stand tall and fight me, then. Use well your last remaining strength.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your wounds give me the upper hand, Melinoë. What hope have you of ever finding our enemy in such a state?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I've not much hope for it at all, Headmistress. Although perhaps I'll get through you, at least.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps, as you say. We fight for every step, for every scrap that we can get. Get through me if you can!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well fought again, Melinoë. Proceed, and moonlight guide you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: May all your enemies beyond succumb to you as readily.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well fought. But I cannot mend your wounds, and neither shall your enemies.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You'll continue to grow, whilst I continue thusly testing you.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Impressive show of force. Hold nothing back as you continue on.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You passed this test again, though how much farther you can go, we'll see.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You've earned these spoils; may they strengthen your craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I shall see you at the Crossroads anon. I shall be catching there my breath...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Whew! No further need to keep each other here, I suppose...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Go on, Melinoë. The greatest teacher is out there.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Just when I thought I had you, you seized control of the fight! Well fought!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You used your last remaining moments very well. May you fare well below.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You wield Descura capably, and I can tell the bond you share is strong. Now farewell.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your growing skill with Lim and Oros makes you something to be feared. Good.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I shall return to shadows hence. Trust that your eve is only beginning.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We shall have to do this again some other eve.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have grown strong, although we've an eternity to hone our craft.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: May you show even greater wrath against the enemies awaiting you below.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well fought, and may the Fates be generous to you in your descent.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I have impeded you for long enough this night. Go, make the most of this attempt.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Flames of Ygnium burn bright for you... but mastery is an everlasting pursuit.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You handle Zorephet as though its bulk is not an inconvenience but a strength...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Skull of Revaal is not a simple artifact to wield... but I can see you two have come to grips.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: At least let me get in a strike or two next time for sake of pride, Melinoë?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: As elusive as a Shade. I don't believe I struck you even once.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Impressive work to have evaded every strike; my own and that of my Sisters as well.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You arrived with not so much as a single cut or scrape, and thus depart. Such strength and agility as can stop Time.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You scarce afforded me a single opening. Show no such mercy to our enemies either.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Swiftly and thoroughly dispatched as though I was no threat to you at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I see you are not in any mood to be slowed down! Hold fast to that ferocity.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Well, go on! 'Tis clear that you are in a hurry, for how quickly you were able to overcome what threat I posed!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Lord Hermes would be most impressed with how swiftly and thoroughly you settled the score here.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Know only that I would not willingly confront the fury of Xinth for merely anyone, Melinoë...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Come show to me what you have lately learned.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Let us begin the lesson, then, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Moon shall be our witness here tonight. Prepare!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Are all your sorceries prepared? Then let us clash.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Strike at me so that the heavens themselves shudder!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Steady your breathing, and steel your nerves, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Hold nothing back against me here, Melinoë.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Each time you face me you step closer to your true potential, Witch.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Now let us match our powers, one Witch to another.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Shall we discern if you can vanquish me again, Witch?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You may surpass me, but how much strength shall doing so cost you?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Come forth, and I expect a better outcome than when last we clashed.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Are you prepared for another hard lesson, or are you going to get past me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You got here quickly, though with haste comes more capacity to make mistakes.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: No getting out of Erebus except through me.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: It seems to me that you are well prepared, so let's begin.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Let us see if you shall keep your streak of victories intact.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Do me the dignity of never letting down your guard.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You have learned much since first we fought, though there is always more.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The pleasantries can wait until we reunite at the Crossroads anon.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I've a sense this shan't take very long at all.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: What say we give Sister Selene a good show up there.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: 'Tis a new moon, and an opportunity for a new beginning. But first...!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You are not merely being tested here; you are never to cease learning.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Moon is full and clear this eve. Brings a certain drama to our confrontation, does it not?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We witches oft are underestimated. But, I am well aware how capable you are.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I can recall some of your many tricks whilst brandishing Descura there, though why don't you remind me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: It's not my custom to let others rush at me with knives. But for you and Lim and Oros, anything.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: So it's to be these old torches of mine against your own once more? I am quite partial to this clash.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: These woods shall look more like a meadow soon as you keep swinging that old Moonstone Axe about...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Argent Skull, the very face of death... I daresay it shows the hint of a smile in our midst.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Xinth the Black Coat once again tonight? Then I shall brace myself for impact, and suggest you do the same.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We each have a Dark Side waiting to reveal itself. Now's as good a time as any to show mine.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Let us ensure that such a terrifying night is not to be forgotten by our witnesses.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I distinctly recall you eluded my every strike in our last outing. Let's see you do that again.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Perhaps you'll do me the courtesy of letting me strike you once or twice this eve, unlike last time...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You achieved victory as swiftly as I've seen when last we clashed. Is that to be your pace from here on out?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Our last confrontation ended practically as soon as it began. Shall you make such short work of me again?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: So, 'tis to be your Dark Side against my own this eve. I shall look forward to seeing the result.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: We each have made an Oath to Night. I expect you to hold nothing back.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: That strange Aspect of the Arms you wield... show me more of what you can achieve with it.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I had expected your arrival here the prior eve, but 'twas not for me to cut short that attempt. However, I shan't hesitate to cut short this one.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You fought with diligence and with ferocity. But surely there is more for you to learn beyond this threshold.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I look forward to it, Headmistress. What about you? Can you not join me?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: No; I dare not stray too far from our abode. Continue now your task. I shall await your safe return.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: ...You've grown. There was a time you never could have stopped me had I used Dark Side against you thus. Hm.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The Arcana gave to you no benefit at all this eve and still you bested me. I know not whether to heap praise, or reprimand you for being such a little show-off! Hah!

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Tell me something now, Melinoë. Who is the fiercest enemy that you have overcome in all of this? Chronos? Typhon?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I know what you insinuate, Headmistress... Hecate. I got in my own way more than any of my foes. Save for you, perhaps, but that's more or less the same thing.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: The limits we impose upon ourselves inhibit what we can achieve. Overcoming those limits is extraordinarily difficult. But, you have done it now... again, and again.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: You've had those flames for quite a while, haven't you, Hecate? Are they... from your place of origin?

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: They lighted my way in my youth, and even still. Care for your possessions, and they may last you a good while...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: I always wondered why none of the Aspects of the Nocturnal Arms I'd discovered were yours...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Try not to dwell on it too much, lest it scatter your mind to no end. But yes, these flames and I do not truly belong. And for that very reason, I suspect the Fates figured me out.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Everything as it once was, yet nothing is the same! A feeling I know only too well. It seems we are to prolong your training for a while...

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My training shall never be complete, isn't that right? Although it's odd to think that following my prior steps to Tartarus shall help maintain the proper course of Time...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Whether we truly possess the capability to maintain Time remains to be seen! But we can at least maintain ourselves.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Your grandfather Chronos like as not awaits beyond this very threshold, where once he stalked that clearing as our bitter enemy. How has collaborating with him been?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Unusual, I'd say... he's proven instrumental in eliminating the remnants of his past self. It must be very strange to face oneself in such a way.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: How very strange indeed. But if it works, I shall not question it too much. Perhaps we'll all learn more of the mysteries of Time ere this is ended.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: When last we fought like this, I posed no real threat to you at all, I know. But your enemies are stubborn; there is nothing you can do to make us stay out of your way.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: All the more reason I would like to make this quick. Though if I'm in a different mood perhaps I'll make things go profoundly worse with just a few Vows from the Oath.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Each night has something of its own to offer. Whether you are concentrating on your swiftness or your strength, ensure you're using well this time we have.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Strength; knowledge; patience. You have exhibited the three repeatedly. Well done.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Thank you, Headmistress. Though I continue learning from our every clash.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Good, because I've no intent to stand aside. Our craft is called a practice for a reason. Now, on your guard.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: You surpassed me before, yet I wonder: Was it your mastery over our craft, or did the Fates themselves perhaps provide a little nudge?

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: Come, Headmistress. If I could be provoked that easily, I'd stand no chance at all. Not even if the Fates desired otherwise.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: Good fortune seldom comes to those in no position to receive it. Show me your victory was no mere accident.

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: So much ado of late, both on Olympus and below. Yet I would have you cast all your worldly troubles aside whilst here.

dialogueLines.speaker: PlayerUnit
dialogueLines.text: My circumstances are what spur me on. Casting them aside shall be difficult, Headmistress...

dialogueLines.speaker: Hecate
dialogueLines.text: I've no concern for difficult. We've time enough to gaze upon our navels at the Crossroads. Here, we are adversaries. Nothing more.
